name: Beliefs & Superstitions
description: The strange laws you follow to keep the darkness at bay.

results.text: Certain trees are sacred and house spirits; you'd never harm one.
Ravens are messengers from the gods; you try to read their caws.
Breaking a bone is a curse that requires an offering to heal.
Collect 'lucky' stones that shine with an inner light.
Wear a particular colour to attract good fortune.
Rain is a blessing from the gods; always pause to offer thanks.
Carry a token of protection against evil spirits.
Clouds are omens shifting with the gods' will.
Always whisper to the land for shelter before making camp.
The left side is sacred; always lead with it in battle.
Crossing a river without a prayer is foolish.
The first animal you see in the morning sets the tone for the day.
Sharp blades have souls; you speak to your weapons with respect.
Low branches are bad luck; never walk beneath them.
Staring into a flame reveals glimpses of the future.
Dreams are messages from ancestors; you record them carefully.
Leave food offerings at the base of trees in the forest.
Sudden chills are warnings from the dead.
Iron is your shield; always carry a small iron nail.
Scars are spirits that linger and protect you.
